headline: Greg Abel’s First Berkshire Letter And What It Signals For Valuation
summary: Greg Abel has issued his first shareholder letter as CEO of Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.A), following Warren Buffett's retirement. The letter marks a major leadership transition at one of the world's largest conglomerates, with investors closely reading Abel's comments on capital allocation and long term priorities. The update comes after a period that included a decline in operating earnings and several high profile portfolio changes across Berkshire's holdings. Berkshire Hathaway enters...

headline: Why Berkshire Hathaway’s Earnings Weren’t as Bad as They Looked
summary: Berkshire Hathaway’s  fourth-quarter operating earnings weren’t great, but they were better than the headline decline of 30%.  Adjust for a $1.56 billion noncash goodwill impairment loss related to four of Berkshire’s subsidiaries, and some other one-time factors, and the drop in operating profits would have been closer to 20%, Barron’s estimates.  Berkshire included that after-tax impairment in its “Other” Income segment of operating income even as it excluded a $4.5 billion noncash write-down of its equity stake in  Occidental Petroleum  from its fourth-quarter operating income.

content.title: Why Berkshire Hathaway’s Earnings Weren’t as Bad as They Looked
content.summary: Berkshire Hathaway’s  fourth-quarter operating earnings weren’t great, but they were better than the headline decline of 30%.  Adjust for a $1.56 billion noncash goodwill impairment loss related to four of Berkshire’s subsidiaries, and some other one-time factors, and the drop in operating profits would have been closer to 20%, Barron’s estimates.  Berkshire included that after-tax impairment in its “Other” Income segment of operating income even as it excluded a $4.5 billion noncash write-down of its equity stake in  Occidental Petroleum  from its fourth-quarter operating income.

headline: Berkshire beyond Buffett: How new CEO Greg Abel's legacy may unfold
summary: Warren Buffett's successor, Greg Abel, faces many challenges as Berkshire Hathaway’s (BRK-A, BRK-B) new CEO, largely having the legacy of Buffett loom over him. Market Catalysts Host Julie Hyman speaks with Suncoast Equity Management managing director and co-portfolio manager Eric Lynch about Abel’s previous influence at the company, and how his own legacy may unfold. Buffett officially handed off the chief executive reins to Abel in the Oracle of Omaha's final shareholder letter over the weekend; Buffett is staying on as chairman of Berkshire's board. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

content.title: Berkshire beyond Buffett: How new CEO Greg Abel's legacy may unfold
content.description:
Warren Buffett's successor, Greg Abel, faces many challenges as Berkshire Hathaway’s (BRK-A, BRK-B) new CEO, largely having the legacy of Buffett loom over him. Market Catalysts Host Julie Hyman speaks with Suncoast Equity Management managing director and co-portfolio manager Eric Lynch about Abel’s previous influence at the company, and how his own legacy may unfold.

Buffett officially handed off the chief executive reins to Abel in the Oracle of Omaha's final shareholder letter over the weekend; Buffett is staying on as chairman of Berkshire's board.

To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

headline: Berkshire Stock Falls 5% as Earnings, Abel Letter Fail to Excite Investors
summary: Berkshire Hathaway’s  fourth-quarter earnings report and CEO Greg Abel’s inaugural shareholder letter are landing with a thud in the stock market Monday as Berkshire stock is down 5%.  Berkshire’s Class A shares are down 4.5% to $723,000, while the B shares are off 4.5% to $482.22.  Berkshire’s operating profits were down 30% in the fourth quarter and even adjusting for some special factors including currency swings and a goodwill write-down, they dropped close to 20%, Barron’s estimates.
